identity.name: The Reservoir Lounge
identity.address: St. Lawrence / Wellington (52 Wellington St E)

narrative.summary: Basement speakeasy near Flatiron. Swing-jazz 5 nights a week. Where Bublé played before fame. The vibe is real — dark, intimate, the musicians are right there. 4.5★ (1391 reviews). $$

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You'll find the entrance on Wellington near the Flatiron, then descend into a low-ceilinged basement where red velvet and dark wood soak up most of the light. The band sets up maybe ten feet from the front tables—no stage separation, no distance—and you'll legitimately need your phone flashlight to read the menu. It's properly dark, properly close, the kind of room where you feel the bass in your sternum and everyone's dressed like they mean it.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The kitchen turns out bistro-style small plates—think the kind of food that won't compete with the music but holds its own. Portions skew tiny, so order multiple dishes if you're actually hungry, and expect to pay moderate prices for the privilege of eating while a six-piece band plays five feet away. This isn't a dining destination; it's fuel for a night when you came for live swing and stayed because the room wouldn't let you leave.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Come on a Friday or Saturday when the full band's playing and you want to dance with strangers who actually know how, or bring someone on a weeknight when the crowd's smaller and you can claim a table near the musicians without a cover charge eating into your bar tab.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Super fun and delicious food. Tiny portion sizes, cover most nights. They had an 80s night when I went which was cool. Get earplugs bc it will be loud. Flashlight needed to look at menu but still loved"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "you're going to find a very classy and groovy vibe in this jazz bar. what an amazing experience!

we were there on friday january 9th for shane philips; him and the band are such gems! the music had lots of people joining the dance floor, and it was so refreshing to be somewhere with a crowd that didn't just stand and look at their phones (personal peeve of mine😅). the crowd was very respectful. everyone was dressed well for the venue and you could tell they were really there for the music 😌

the food and service were a bit lacklustre (it seemed our server had a big section that was quickly sat all at once), but it is not the main event. i did enjoy their oysters though! the drinks were great and my partner really enjoyed their lychee mocktail.

i would 100% recommend making a reservation as it gets busy early on. i received prompt replies to my email request (contacted through their website) and during opening hours i was able to make a phone call to make a change to my reso. be advised there is a cash only cover charge at the door (ours was $15 per person).

the last time i was here was a couple years ago, and returning on friday night made me realize what i've been missing out on. i will definitely be coming back."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "While in search of a jazz/blues bar near the Eaton Centre, we came across The Reservoir Lounge. And boy, are we glad we did!

Seating wasn’t available without a reservation, but we got there about 30 mins before the show started and got a great standing spot at the bar.

Cover charge at the door (I think it was $15 a person), free coat check was available, and the bar service was pretty good considering how busy it was.

The drinks were just okay, my old fashioned was weak, but who cares when the music and the atmosphere breaks the scale! Definitely a must-try while in Toronto"
